facts.text: Rockville Centre got its name in 1849 from local Methodist preacher Mordecai "Rock" Smith.
The town’s post office, built in 1850, sits on the site of Robert Pettit’s original general store and is listed on the National Register.
The Rockville Centre station opened in 1873 and still serves commuters on the Long Island Railroad’s Main Line.
Agnes, the cathedral church of the Diocese of Rockville Centre, is a striking landmark noted for its architecture and role in Long Island Catholic history.
Anne Meara, a celebrated actress and comedian, once called Rockville Centre home before becoming the mother of actor Ben Stiller.
The Rockville Centre Historical Society preserves local history, sharing stories from the 19th‑century general store to the town’s modern community.
